title: laserpitium latifolium var. asperum
intro: Laserpitium latifolium var. asperum, also known as the rough-hairy laserpitium, is a plant species within the Carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a carminative, diuretic, and expectorant, helping to relieve symptoms of indigestion, urinary issues, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Laserpitium latifolium var. asperum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of fever and cough. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, with the roots and leaves being the most commonly used parts.
